text: Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks: Evidence from China’s Primary Land Market Jinghong Li (Southern Asset Management) Kevin Zhengcheng Liu (Zhejiang University) Zhuo Nie (UIBE) Jiaqi Zhao (Peking University) 29th Annual SIOE Conference August 25, 2025 Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 1 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Motivation • Global economy confronts heightened trade shocks, after decades of globalization • Trade openness increases the exposure to external economic shocks and risk • The decline of jobs and wage • Political and social consequences: populism, political polarization, crime, and protests • Government plays a crucial risk-mitigating role under globalization • Less is known about the local government’s response to trade shocks Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 2 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Funding problem for fiscal stimulus • Active local fiscal response is challenging in economic downturns 1 Tax revenues are procyclical: negative shocks ↓revenues 2 Spending/deficit is subject to the predetermined budget • One of such strategies: selling land to the private sector — “land finance” • In developing countries (e.g., China, Vietnam, India, Africa) and 19th–20th-century US • Recent proposal (alive debate) of massive federal land sale-off in US detail • Yet, land finance also involves the firm buyers’ decision • Firm-gov’t/politician connections may play a crucial role • Politicians may exert influence on connected firms and sell land to them • Connected firms grant favor to gov’ts Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 3 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix This paper Studies the fiscal impacts of trade shocks, provides evidence on the existence and implications of a novel firm-politician connection channel of land sale revenues • Context: China’s marked export slowdown during mid-2010s • Export-led development; but experiencing a rapid export slowdown recently Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 4 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Background: China’s export slowdown • Striking decline from the average annual export growth rate of 18% from 1992-2008 • The ongoing trade war with the US in 2025 further underscores the heightened risks 0 5 10 15 20 25 Export Growth Rate (%) 2010 2013 2016 2019 Year Export Growth Fitted Line Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 5 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix This paper Studies the fiscal impacts of trade shocks, provides evidence on the existence and implications of a novel firm-politician connection channel of land sale revenues • Context: China’s marked export slowdown during mid-2010s • Export-led development, but experiencing a rapid export slowdown recently • Local gov’ts rely on land sales to fund infrastructure investment Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 6 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Background: the prominent role of land revenues in municipal budget • Land revenues amount to 70%–100% of all other tax revenues 0 3000 6000 9000 12000 Billion Yuan 0 20 40 60 80 100 Share (%) 2010 2013 2016 2019 Year Land Sale Revenue to Local General Fiscal Revenue (Left Axis) Land Sale Revenue to Local Tax Revenue (Left Axis) Land Sale Revenue (Right Axis) Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 7 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix This paper Studies the fiscal impacts of trade shocks, provides evidence on the existence and implications of a novel firm-politician connection channel of land sale revenues • Context: China’s marked export slowdown during mid-2010s • Export-led development; but experiencing rapid export slowdown recently • Local gov’ts rely on land sales to fund infrastructure investment • Primary land market: city gov’ts (as monopolistic seller) have large discretion over land supply; prone to corruption Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 8 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix This paper Studies the fiscal impacts of trade shocks, provides evidence on the existence and implications of a novel firm-politician connection channel of land sale revenues • Context: China’s marked export slowdown during mid-2010s • Export-led development; but experiencing rapid export slowdown recently • Local gov’ts rely on land sales to fund infrastructure investment • Primary land market: city gov’ts (as monopolistic seller) have large discretion over land supply; prone to corruption • Identification similar to Autor, Dorn, & Hanson • Instrument for the export shock: a Bartik-style variable, constructed from (i) a city’s initial product-level export shares; (ii) rest-of-world product-level trade shocks Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 8 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Finding preview • Negative export shock →active land finance response by local governments • Cities exposed to export slowdowns ↑land sale revenues • Driven by (non-local) connected firm buyers • Connected firm’s favoring/contributive motives, rather than bottom fishing • Connected firms buy more land at higher prices • Heterogeneous effects consistent with politicians building ties with firms • Fiscal implications: export shock associated with • Stimulus spending ↑, mild effect on overall revenue and expenditure, null effect on GDP • Suggestive evidence on returns to connected firms (quid pro quo) Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 9 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Related literature 1 Social and economic impacts of trade shocks • local labor market effects; social consequences, strikes, and crime; political effects 2 Economic shocks and local public finance • Natural disasters, climate change, pandemics, pension deficits, municipal credit constraints 3 Political economy of fiscal spending • Electoral motives and corporate political connections affect stimulus measures 4 Connections between firm and government • Firms receive benefits from governments • Politicians receive benefits from firms Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 10 / 25
Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Data and method Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 11 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Empirical specification data source City-year level first difference regression: ∆Yct = • Export shock (Per worker in 1,000 USD) measure for t = 2010,2011,.,2019 ExpShockct = X k ∆Xckt Lc • Xckt: export value of HS 6-digit product k by city c in year t • Lc: working-age population in 2005 • • Weight by prefecture working age population in 2010 • Standard errors are clustered at the city level Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 12 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Shift-share IV strategy • Shift-share instrumental variable ExpShockROW ct = X k Xck,2009 P c Xck,2009 ∆XROW kt Lc • XROW kt: total exports of product k by the rest of the world (ROW) except China • Xck,2009 P c Xck,2009: importance of city c in exporting product k in the pre-sample year • Validity of shift-share IV for ExpShockct • Balance tests: Shifts in product-level trade shocks are as good as randomly assigned to prefectures • Pre-trend test: ExpShockROWc,t+1 has no impact on the current outcomes Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 13 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Measure firm-politician/local gov’t connections • Leveraging a rotation policy in China’s political system • We consider a firm X located in city A as (likely) connected with the gov’t of city B, if leaders transfer from city A →city B detail • Defined by dynamic directed networks between cities by rotation of city leaders • Granular firm-city pairs level spatial + time variation in the connection status • Has been shown to have substantial impacts on firms’ cross-city investments and land purchase Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 14 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix The role of firm-gov’t connections: effect by firm types • Adverse export shock→↓sales from local firms, but more revenues from non-local firms • Particularly strong for firms connected to city leaders (↑88 % or 0. billion RMB) Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 18 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Connected firms’ favoring motives: land characteristics • Land transaction details (area-weighted city-level average) local polynomial • Under local economic downturns, connected firms buy more land at a higher price • No evidence on change in land quality →not bottom fishing in the land market Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 19 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Connected firms’ favoring motives: heterogeneity in connection building • Exploit variation in the leader’s last serving experiences when she formed ties with firms • Stronger effects if leaders’ last serving cities: table • Have more locally promoted leaders →deeper relationship with firms • High frequency of visiting firms →more opportunity to build ties with local firms • Real estate sector is dominated by private firms →larger pool of connected firms Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 20 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Effects on stimulus measures and fiscal outcomes • Increased land revenues fund stimulus measures under negative shocks →Mild effect on GDP, night light, and fiscal outcome • Consistent with ↑firm entry in the construction and real estate sector table • Firm level evidence: ↑in sales, investment, tax payment table Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 21 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Consequences on firm’s real outcomes Firm-level event study around favoring land purchase, using National Tax Survey Data: Yit = • Favoring land purchase: firm’s first land purchase in a politically connected city when the city was experiencing a negative export shock • Postk it: relative event-time dummies (k year before or after favoring land purchase) • • Restrict sample to firms having ever purchased land parcels Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 22 / 25

Introduction Data and method Main results Fiscal implications appendix Conclusions • We study the fiscal response to export shocks in Chinese cities from 2009-2019 • We find that: • China’s export slowdown leads to increased land sale revenues • A novel firm-politician connection channel in land finance: local politicians raise land revenues from connected (non-local) firms to fund proactive stimulus • As a result, export slowdown is associated with (i) increased stimulus investment; (ii) increased firm investment; (iii) mild impacts on economy and municipal budget • Sheds light on the nuance of firm-government relationship in China • Far from “arm’s length relationships” with a complete formal institution • Not pure “quo-pro quid” for private profit • “Private firms, public interests” Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 25 / 25
Appendix Slides Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 1 / 8

The (Ongoing) Return of Land Finance in the US? back • Land finance played a crucial role in US during the early 20th century • “Federal lands and resources have played a significant role in American history, adding to the strength and stature of the federal government.. and providing a source of revenue for schools, transportation, national defense, and other national, state, and local needs. ” • The federal government now owns 640 million acres (28% of the nation’s land) • House Republicans have approved the sale of 10,000 acres of federal land in Nevada and Utah on May 7, 2025 • DOI Secretary Doug Burgum: “Lands are an incredible asset on America’s balance sheet.” • Intended to fund the One Big Beautiful Bill Act and the sovereign wealth fund Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 2 / 8

Key data source back • Unit of observations: Chinese cities (prefectures) from 2009 to 2019 • Trade data • Annual exports data at HS6 product level from China’s Customs • World trade data at the country-HS6 product level from CEPII-BACI database • Administrative data on the universe of primary market land parcel transactions from the Ministry of Land and Resources • Matched with Business Registration data • Other city-level economic and fiscal variables from several City Statistical Yearbooks • Local annual budget data • Firm-level data from National Tax Survey Database Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 3 / 8

Firm-politician connection: details back • Focus on five types of politicians • City mayor and party secretary — prefectural rank • Members of provincial party standing committee — sub-provincial rank • Provincial governor / party secretary — provincial rank • Connection definition: • Rotation from city gov A to B: firms in city A →city B • Rotation from provincial position M to N: firms in province M →all cities in province N • Rotation from provincial position M to city B: firms in province M →city B • Subsidiary firms • If firm X located in city A is connected with the city B →local firm Y (in city B) whose largest shareholder is firm X is also connected with city B Li, Liu, Nie & Zhao Firm-Politician Nexus and Local Public Finance under Trade Shocks 4 / 8
